decisions.title: Enable Shadow Traffic in Production Environments
decisions.context: ENG-10486 ready to enable shadow traffic/comparisons in prod and gov. This validates data_layer_v3 architecture before full cutover, critical for Nail the Core pillar and system reliability.

arguments.for: Validates v3 architecture in real conditions, reduces cutover risk, enables data-driven migration decisions. Shadow mode = zero customer impact.
arguments.against: Adds infra cost, potential for monitoring noise, requires investigation bandwidth if divergence found (see ENG-10539 dev results).

decisions.title: Default Min/Max Sampling for Numeric Channels
decisions.context: ENG-10497 proposes making min/max default sampling method for numeric channels. Affects core Explore V2 UX and signal quality perception. Relates to 'downsampled' status confusion in ENG-10556.

arguments.for: Preserves peak values critical for anomaly detection, aligns with user expectations for engineering data, reduces 'why did I miss that spike' support tickets.
arguments.against: Changes established behavior, may surprise users expecting average sampling, increases backend processing load for high-frequency channels.

decisions.title: Canvas Make Copy Feature Scope
decisions.context: ENG-9574 adds copy functionality to Canvas. Need decision on scope: copy-in-place vs workspace-level vs org-level, and whether to include version history.

arguments.for: Workspace-level copy enables template workflows, accelerates 'Embed Deeper' by enabling teams to share analysis patterns. Version history copy = audit trail.
arguments.against: Org-level copy risks content sprawl, version history increases storage costs, need clear permissions model before expanding scope.

decisions.title: Calculated Channel Combinatoric Explosion Resolution
decisions.context: ENG-10185 reports calc channel combinatoric explosion on asset. This impacts performance at scale, affects 'Build the Moat' if competitors handle complex calcs better.
decisions.blockedBy: Need profiling data to determine if issue is evaluation-time or definition-time, and whether caching or lazy evaluation is the right pattern.

arguments.for: Solving enables complex multi-asset analysis, unlocks fleet-level calculated channels, differentiator for sophisticated customers.
arguments.against: May require fundamental calc engine redesign, could delay other V2 features, workarounds exist for most use cases.

decisions.title: Ask SIFT Thinking Event UI Pattern
decisions.context: ENG-10520 implements frontend for 'Thinking Event'. Decision needed: show reasoning inline vs expandable vs summary-only. Affects Signal>Evidence>Decision transparency.

arguments.for: Expandable thinking UI builds trust through transparency, enables users to verify reasoning, teaches them to ask better questions. Aligns with Evidence pillar.
arguments.against: Adds visual clutter, risks overwhelming non-technical users, may expose model limitations/errors. Summary-only is cleaner UX.

decisions.title: Family Member Run Count Hard Limit
decisions.context: ENG-9568 proposes hard limit on family member run count. Need decision on limit value and behavior when exceeded. Affects multi-run comparison workflows in Explore V2.

arguments.for: Prevents performance degradation from 500+ run families, forces users toward better data organization patterns, protects system stability.
arguments.against: May break existing customer workflows, arbitrary limit feels wrong for 'unlimited data' positioning, better solution is pagination/virtualization.
